channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Damaged apvement, 28th May
item.description:
Pavement on Church Row being damaged by lorries accessing building site at No.12A Tyre tracks on opposite pavement, outside 13/13A: part has been damaged
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Church Row, West Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead fox cub under car on the road, 28th May
item.description:
It’s a poor dead fox in a blood bath that needs to be dignitously removed

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woodhill, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Vehicle vehicle left for weeks, 28th May
item.description:
Ford Kuga MX22 TUV has been been parked in this spot for weeks now and hasn’t moved. It is parked opposite number 29 The Drive. The Vehicle vehicle is untaxed. I have reported to DVLA but there has been no response. Parking is extremely limited in this road, which often results in people Parking dangerous dangerously on corners if there aren’t enough spaces.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 32, The Drive, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Using communal bins to dump building waste, 28th May
item.description:
21 woodrow are constantly using this area to dump there building waste instead of getting a skip

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woodhill, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Building collapse, 28th May
item.description:
Part of the building has fallen! This could have killed someone!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 8, Masons Hill, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 59 hartland, 28th May
item.description:
Chair

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hartland Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Dead fox, 28th May
item.description:
Theres a dead fox lying on the grass outside Lawson house. Its rotting in the sun and smells really bad. You can see it from the pavement. Near the bus stop for the 244.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Nightingale Place, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street weeds throughout, 28th May
item.description:
As above, throughout kerb line of road, picked up during routine inspection

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Edmund Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 88 Brondesbury road, 28th May
item.description:
Stool

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Tree on footpath, 28th May
item.description:
This tree was on the road outside our house/Baylis place. We suspect it may have fallen off a truck. It has not come from our trees. We have pulled it off road to pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bickley Road, Bickley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hazelmere, 28th May
item.description:
Wood panels

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woodville Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Fly-tipping area, 28th May
item.description:
Hi there, This area and opposite the road have constantly fly-tipping issue. Please collect the rubbish and instal camera or a least a sign there. Best, Lidia

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolwich Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Ongoing Fly-tipping, 28th May
item.description:
Hi there, At the bottom of Victoria Way, there is a constant fly-tipping spot. Can this be addressed by the council asap? There should be camera installed or at least a sign about fly-tipping fines. Best Lidia

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolwich Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: By the bins and along the fence, 28th May
item.description:
Since 1 week

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Gollogly Terrace, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tip, 28th May
item.description:
Metal exercise equipment

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Burrage Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overleaking bin with stones and dirty sanitary towels has not been emptied for more than a month, 28th May
item.description:
The bin that is full next to St Vincent house today is full of hair and dirty sanitary towels and there are even more rubbish around it scattered on the floor, making it non-hygienic LP and dangerous. Please empty as soon as possible. Apparently there are heavy items and stones inside the bin.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Fendall Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grass neds reseeding and tree planting, 28th May
item.description:
Verge destroyed by building works at 20 and cars parking on grass. Needs reseeding. Tree needs planting - only verge on street without tree.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Yester Road, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped fridge and sofas, 28th May
item.description:
Fridge dumped and sofas not picked up yet. The usual Thanks team!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 210, Venner Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing bins, 28th May
item.description:
Overflowing bin being pecked at by crows

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Aldermans Hill, Southgate
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter, broken glass, and feces in the alleyway, 28th May
item.description:
There is an orange bag with perishable rubbish left in the alleyway between Fairmead (number 19) and Springfield Gardens. It has been there for more than a week. There is other litter, broken glass bottles, and feces on the pathway as well. Could you please clear it. It is frequently used by older people and children. The glass is especially dangerous. This alleyway and surrounding woodland are used as a dumping ground for rubbish. Could it be please regularly cleaned. There are large items left there (e.g. artificial turf on Faimead close side) that have been there for long time.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Fairmead, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
